messages.content: You are a Lean 4 autoformalization agent working in a current Mathlib-only project. Avoid stale Lean 3/old Mathlib syntax and identifiers; follow the lean_environment/current_version_guidance and local Lean examples. Return exactly one JSON object.

    "Every nonstandard helper theorem or local API used in the proof should appear explicitly in the Lean prefix context or local examples. If it is not displayed, do not use its name.",

    "If an existing theorem or lemma in the prefix context has binders, apply it to the needed variables rather than using the theorem constant bare.",
    "Prefer the narrowest theorem directly supported by the source sentence; avoid generalizing to a stronger forall/if statement unless that exact shape is in the source or prefix context.",
    "Prefer a short proof if the prefix context already contains the needed fact."
  ],
  "context": {
    "source_statement_or_chunk": [
      {
        "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/SymmetricFunctions/LittlewoodRichardson.tex",

        "labels": [
          "lem.sf.alternant-0"
        ],
        "snippet": "\\begin{lemma}\n\\label{lem.sf.alternant-0}Let $\\alpha\\in\\mathbb{N}^{N}$. \\medskip\n\n\\textbf{(a)} If the $N$-tuple $\\alpha$ has two equal entries, then $a_{\\alpha\n}=0$. \\medskip\n\n\\textbf{(b)} Let $\\beta\\in\\mathbb{N}^{N}$ be an $N$-tuple obtained from\n$\\alpha$ by swapping two entries. Then, $a_{\\beta}=-a_{\\alpha}$.\n\\end{lemma}\n\n\\begin{proof}\n[Proof of Lemma \\ref{lem.sf.alternant-0}.]This is an easy consequence of\nDefinition \\ref{def.sf.alternants} \\textbf{(b)}. See Section\n\\ref{sec.details.sf.schur} for a detailed proof.\n\\end{proof}\n\n\\begin{proof}\n[Proof of Lemma \\ref{lem.sf.stemb-lem}.]For any $\\beta\\in\\mathbb{N}^{N}$ and\nany $i\\in\\left[  N\\right]  $, we let $\\beta_{i}$ denote the $i$-th entry of\n$\\beta$. Thus, for example, $\\rho_{k}=N-k$ for each $k\\in\\left[  N\\right]  $\n(since $\\rho=\\left(  N-1,N-2,\\ldots,N-N\\right)  $).\n\nSince addition on $\\mathbb{N}^{N}$ is defined entrywise, we have $\\left(\n\\beta+\\gamma\\right)  _{i}=\\beta_{i}+\\gamma_{i}$ for any $\\beta,\\gamma\n\\in\\mathbb{N}^{N}$ and $i\\in\\left[  N\\right]  $.\n\nThe group $S_{N}$ acts on $\\mathcal{P}$ by $K$-algebra automorphisms. Hence,\nin particular, we have%\n\\[\n\\sigma\\cdot\\left(  fg\\right)  =\\left(  \\sigma\\cdot f\\right)  \\cdot\\left(\n\\sigma\\cdot g\\right)\n\\]\nfor any $\\sigma\\in S_{N}$ and any $f,g\\in\\mathcal{P}$. In other words, we have%\n\\begin{equation}\n\\left(  \\sigma\\cdot f\\right)  \\cdot\\left(  \\sigma\\cdot g\\right)  =\\sigma\n\\cdot\\left(  fg\\right)  \\label{pf.lem.sf.stemb-lem.sigfg=}%\n\\end{equation}\nfor any $\\sigma\\in S_{N}$ and any $f,g\\in\\mathcal{P}$.\n"
      }
    ],

          "doc_excerpt": "An alternant is zero if two entries of α are equal.\nLemma \\ref{lem.sf.alternant-0}(a) in the source.\n\nThis delegates to `AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant_eq_zero_of_repeated`.\ntheorem alternant_zero_of_eq {R : Type*} [CommRing R] {α : Fin N → ℕ}\n(i j : Fin N) (hij : i ≠ j) (heq : α i = α j) :\nalternant (R := R) N α = 0 :=\nAlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant_eq_",
          "declaration_head": "/-- An alternant is zero if two entries of α are equal.\n    Lemma \\ref{lem.sf.alternant-0}(a) in the source.\n\n    This delegates to `AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant_eq_zero_of_repeated`. -/\ntheorem alternant_zero_of_eq {R : Type*} [CommRing R] {α : Fin N → ℕ}\n    (i j : Fin N) (hij : i ≠ j) (heq : α i = α j) :\n    alternant (R",

          "doc_excerpt": "If α has two equal entries, then a_α = 0. (lem.sf.alternant-0 (a))\ntheorem alternant_eq_zero_of_repeated {α : Fin N → ℕ}\n(h : ∃ i j : Fin N, i ≠ j ∧ α i = α j) :\n(alternant α : MvPolynomial (Fin N) R) = 0 := by\nobtain ⟨i, j, hij, hαij⟩ := h\nunfold alternant\napply Finset.sum_involution (fun σ _ => Equiv.swap i j * σ)\n· -- f(σ) + f(swap * σ) = 0\nintro σ _\nsimp only [Equiv.Perm.sign_mul, Equiv.Perm.sign_swap hij]\nrw [show α ∘ (Equiv.swap i j * σ) = α ∘ σ from ?_]\n· simp [add_neg",
          "declaration_head": "/-- If α has two equal entries, then a_α = 0. (lem.sf.alternant-0 (a)) -/\ntheorem alternant_eq_zero_of_repeated {α : Fin N → ℕ}\n    (h : ∃ i j : Fin N, i ≠ j ∧ α i = α j) :\n    (alternant α : MvPolynomial (Fin N) R) = 0",
          "why_candidate": "Imported project declaration references the visible source label.",

        },

          "doc_excerpt": "Swapping two entries of α negates the alternant. (lem.sf.alternant-0 (b))\ntheorem alternant_swap {α : Fin N → ℕ} {i j : Fin N} (hij : i ≠ j) :\n(alternant (α ∘ Equiv.swap i j) : MvPolynomial (Fin N) R) = -alternant α := by\nsimp only [alternant]\n-- Reindex the sum using the bijection σ ↦ swap i j * σ\n-- Key: (α ∘ swap i j) ∘ σ = α ∘ (swap i j * σ)\n-- After substituting τ = swap i j * σ (equivalently σ = swap i j * τ since swap is self-inverse):\n-- ∑_τ sign(swap * τ) • xPow(α ∘ ",
          "declaration_head": "/-- Swapping two entries of α negates the alternant. (lem.sf.alternant-0 (b)) -/\ntheorem alternant_swap {α : Fin N → ℕ} {i j : Fin N} (hij : i ≠ j) :\n    (alternant (α ∘ Equiv.swap i j) : MvPolynomial (Fin N) R) = -alternant α",

If an earlier declaration already formalizes one part, prefer the remaining or next source part instead of bundling all parts. Use imported_source_label_declarations as previous formalized project statements when they match the selected source part; prefer applying an imported theorem over reproving its full internal proof.",

    "lean_prefix_context": "-- File context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/SchurBasics.lean:43-43 (section)\nnoncomputable section\n\n-- File context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/SchurBasics.lean:45-45 (open)\nopen Finset Matrix Polynomial MvPolynomial BigOperators\n\n-- File context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/SchurBasics.lean:47-47 (variable)\nvariable (N : ℕ) [NeZero N]\n\n-- File context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/SchurBasics.lean:139-139 (variable)\nvariable {R : Type*} [CommRing R]\n\n-- Predecessor context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/SchurBasics.lean:141-150\n/-- The alternant a_α for an N-tuple α.\n    Definition \\ref{def.sf.alternants}(b) in the source.\n\n    This is an abbreviation for `AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant` from LittlewoodRichardson.lean,\n    which defines it as ∑_{σ ∈ S_N} sign(σ) · x^(σ·α). This equals the determinant-based\n    definition det(x_j^{α_i}) by `AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant_eq_det`.\n\n    The explicit `N` parameter is kept for backward compatibility with existing code. -/\nnoncomputable abbrev alternant (N : ℕ) (α : Fin N → ℕ) : MvPolynomial (Fin N) R :=\n  AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant α\n\n-- Predecessor context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/LittlewoodRichardson.lean:405-406\nnoncomputable abbrev xPow (α : Fin N → ℕ) : MvPolynomial (Fin N) R :=\n  AlgebraicCombinatorics.SymmetricFunctions.monomialExp α\n\n-- Predecessor context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/LittlewoodRichardson.lean:859-867\n/-- The alternant a_α = ∑_{σ ∈ S_N} sign(σ) · x^(σ·α).\n    Here σ·α means (α_{σ(1)}, α_{σ(2)}, ..., α_{σ(N)}).\n\n    **Equivalence with SchurBasics.alternant:**\n    This sum-based definition equals the determinant-based definition in\n    `alternant` (from SchurBasics.lean). See `alternant_eq_det` for the proof\n    that this equals `det(alternantMatrix α)`. -/\nnoncomputable def alternant (α : Fin N → ℕ) : MvPolynomial (Fin N) R :=\n  ∑ σ : Equiv.Perm (Fin N), Equiv.Perm.sign σ • xPow (α ∘ σ)\n\n-- Predecessor context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/LittlewoodRichardson.lean:1045-1099\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/SchurBasics.lean:152-157 (alternantTextbook)\n/-- Alternative definition matching the textbook convention exactly:\n    entry (i, j) = x_i^{α_j}. This equals `alternant` since det(A) = det(A^T). -/\ndef alternantTextbook (N : ℕ) (α : Fin N → ℕ) : MvPolynomial (Fin N) R :=\n  Matrix.det (Matrix.of fun i j => (X i : MvPolynomial (Fin N) R) ^ α j)\n\nomit [NeZero N] in\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/SchurBasics.lean:158-167 (alternantTextbook_eq_alternant)\n/-- The two alternant definitions are equal.\n    This uses `AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant_eq_det` to bridge the sum-based\n    alternant definition with the determinant-based alternantTextbook. -/\ntheorem alternantTextbook_eq_alternant (α : Fin N → ℕ) :\n    alternantTextbook (R := R) N α = alternant N α := by\n  unfold alternantTextbook alternant\n  rw [AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant_eq_det]\n  unfold AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternantMatrix\n  rw [← Matrix.det_transpose]\n  congr 1\n-- Same-file source-label API: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/SchurBasics.lean:3073-3082 (alternant_zero_of_eq)\n/-- An alternant is zero if two entries of α are equal.\n    Lemma \\ref{lem.sf.alternant-0}(a) in the source.\n\n    This delegates to `AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant_eq_zero_of_repeated`. -/\ntheorem alternant_zero_of_eq {R : Type*} [CommRing R] {α : Fin N → ℕ}\n    (i j : Fin N) (hij : i ≠ j) (heq : α i = α j) :\n    alternant (R := R) N α = 0 :=\n  AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant_eq_zero_of_repeated ⟨i, j, hij, heq⟩\n\nomit [NeZero N] in",

        "If the local examples use a domain-specific API such as `Ring.choose`, `updateCol`, or a local theorem name, keep that exact API family instead of falling back to a more familiar but different API such as `Nat.choose` or `Matrix.updateColumn`."

      "examples": [
        "/-- The two alternant definitions are equal.\n    This uses `AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant_eq_det` to bridge the sum-based\n    alternant definition with the determinant-based alternantTextbook. -/\ntheorem alternantTextbook_eq_alternant (α : Fin N → ℕ) :\n    alternantTextbook (R := R) N α = alternant N α := by\n  unfold alternantTextbook alternant\n  rw [AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant_eq_det]\n  unfold AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternantMatrix\n  rw [← Matrix.det_transpose]\n  congr 1"

      "Source focus: Formalize Lemma lem.sf.alternant-0: (a) If the N-tuple α has two equal entries, then a_α = 0. (b) If β is obtained from α by swapping two entries, then a_β = -a_α.",
      "Selected source part: Part (b) of Lemma lem.sf.alternant-0: swapping two entries of α negates the alternant.",

The prefix context shows `prior_same_label_declarations` includes `alternant_zero_of_eq` for part (a). Therefore, only part (b) remains to be formalized.",
      "Formalization sketch:",
      "- Statement: theorem alternant_swap' {α : Fin N → ℕ} {i j : Fin N} (hij : i ≠ j) : alternant (R := R) N (α ∘ Equiv.swap i j) = -alternant N α",
      "- Proof: Use the definition of `alternant` as a sum over permutations. Reindex the sum by multiplying each σ by the transposition (i j). Use the sign change of the permutation and the fact that (α ∘ swap i j) ∘ σ = α ∘ (swap i j * σ).",

      "- `Equiv.Perm.sign_swap`; shape: sign_swap {α : Type*} [DecidableEq α] {x y : α} (h : x ≠ y) : Equiv.Perm.sign (Equiv.swap x y) = -1; why: The sign of a transposition is -1, used in the proof.; confidence: 0.95",

      "- Whether the local `alternant` abbreviation is exactly `AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant` or has additional arguments. The prefix context shows `noncomputable abbrev alternant (N : ℕ) (α : Fin N → ℕ) : MvPolynomial (Fin N) R := AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant α`, so it's a direct abbreviation.",
      "Hydrated Mathlib source snippets:",
      "Mathlib source match for `Equiv.Perm.sign_swap` at Mathlib/GroupTheory/Perm/Sign.lean:388:\n```lean\ntheorem sign_swap {x y : α} (h : x ≠ y) : sign (swap x y) = -1 :=\n  (signAux3_mul_and_swap 1 1 _ mem_univ).2 h\n\n@[simp]\n```",
      "Mathlib source match for `Equiv.Perm.sign_swap` at Mathlib/GroupTheory/Perm/Sign.lean:392:\n```lean\ntheorem sign_swap' {x y : α} : sign (swap x y) = if x = y then 1 else -1 :=\n  if H : x = y then by simp [H, swap_self] else by simp [sign_swap H, H]\n```",
      "Mathlib source match for `Equiv.swap` at Mathlib/Data/TwoPointing.lean:53:\n```lean\ndef swap : TwoPointing α :=\n  ⟨(p.snd, p.fst), p.snd_ne_fst⟩\n```",
      "Mathlib source match for `Equiv.swap` at Mathlib/Topology/Algebra/ContinuousMonoidHom.lean:223:\n```lean\ndef swap : (A × B) →ₜ* (B × A) := prod (snd A B) (fst A B)\n\nsection CommMonoid\nvariable [CommMonoid E] [TopologicalSpace E] [ContinuousMul E]\n\n/-- The continuous homomorphism given by multiplication. -/\n@[to_additive (attr := simps!) /-- The continuous homomorphism given by addition. -/]\n```",
